description: A path that takes much walking is 'laeno'; so is an interval that takes much waiting. The same adjective covers spatial length and temporal duration. Vertical height is 'raelu' (tall), while 'teku' (short) marks limited extent on the end-to-end scale.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and open into central 'a'. Release that vowel before a separate stressed front 'e'. The tongue tip then moves to the upper teeth for dental nasal 'n' before rounded mid-back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The vowel break holds 'laeno' across three distinct syllables. The word takes its time through the hiatus, much like the path or interval it describes.
usage_notes: A long path is 'laeno ruela'; a long time is 'laeno thimu'. Context supplies the comparison and any exact measurement.

semantic_domains.spatial: substantial end-to-end extent in a path or object
semantic_domains.temporal: a duration that continues for a substantial interval
